rows.who: Runs fak serve in production: wants a container image, metrics, health checks, resource limits, and a deployment story.
rows.job: Deploy fak serve with /metrics, /healthz, rate limits, and a container image — and a guide for Docker, Compose, and k8s.

affordances.need: a root Dockerfile that builds fak to a minimal/distroless image
a production deployment guide (Docker / Compose / k8s / bare metal) with a readiness checklist
the deploy guide documents the Prometheus /metrics and /healthz endpoints
an observability scorecard that proves dashboards/alerts/metrics-doc stay honest
a server-config reference (flags + env vars) for production tuning
a throughput/cost governor (rate limiting) so a runaway agent can't blow the budget
a copy-paste k8s manifest / Helm chart, not just a guide section

gaps: Ships a raw k8s manifest (deploy/k8s/, apply with kubectl -k), not a Helm chart — Helm is a future option
No root docker-compose.yml — Compose is documented inline in the guide

rows.who: Evaluates fak's capability-floor and containment claims before trusting it in front of an agent; reads the threat model and the refusal vocabulary.
rows.job: Audit what fak denies/contains, confirm the refusals are a closed vocabulary, the audit trail is tamper-evident, and the CI scans for CVEs.

affordances.need: a threat model that states what is in scope and what is out (detector evasion is out by design)
a security-audit workflow (govulncheck + posture/leak audit) on push/PR/schedule
a CLOSED refusal vocabulary so a deny cites a code, never free text
the deployable capability-floor spec (the dump→edit→check→load policy workflow)
an honesty ledger where every claim carries one status tag
security-review tooling (CI wiring + secret-leak scanner checks)

gaps: No third-party security audit / pentest report yet — the posture is self-attested via SECURITY.md + the CI gates
